行了，快走吧。禁区本来就不是任你游荡的地方。
All right, you may leave now. The restricted zone is not a hangout.
可…可恶…这家伙，有点本事！不是一般的破坏狂。
You... You're pretty tough for a vandal!
唔…可恶啊！居然连最基本的公物都保护不了，真是狼狈…真是愧对邓恩队长…！
I can't even protect the most basic infrastructure... I've brought shame to Captain Dunn!
打碎容器是为了保护和平。
I broke the vessels to preserve peace.
居然能面不改色心不跳地说出这种话…看来你确实不是一般人！
You got to be something else to say that with a straight face!
Captain Dunn isn't a match for me, either.
…你和邓恩队长交过手？难怪…连队长都没能战胜的对手，我一个列兵又有什么机会？是我自不量力了……
You fought Captain Dunn? No wonder... How can a soldier like me beat someone even the captain couldn't defeat? You're way above my league...
No one can stop me! No one!
居然以破坏公物为乐…该死！我怎么会输给这种人……
How I can lose to someone who relishes in the destruction of public property?
唉，算了…拳头不够硬，说什么都是白搭！既然拦不住你，那就只能由你去了……
Oh, forget it. I can't beat you, so do what you will...
但是，你给我记住——有朝一日，等我修炼归来…我一定会为被无端破坏的补给容器们报仇！你给我等着！
But I'll be back one day to avenge the broken supply vessel. Just you wait!
哈，刚才你不是很嚣张吗？怎么，这就被打服了？
Not so tough now, are you?
这次就当是一次警告——以后你再路过补给容器的时候，最好给我多长个心眼！
Take this as a warning to never mess with another supply vessel again!
好了，赶紧从我眼前消失…铁卫禁区不欢迎你这种家伙。
Now remove yourself from my sight! The Silvermane Guards don't want vandals like you in the restricted zone!
这是一只造型古典的皮质手提箱，表面落了一层厚厚的灰尘。旅行箱会出现在车站，可以说是再正常不过…但它属于正常状况中相对异常的那种。

This leather suitcase has a vintage design, with layer upon layer of dust piling up on top of it. Typically, seeing such luggage at a transportation station is nothing unusual. So it can be said that this suitcase's lonely presence still falls within the realm of what is considered normal, even though it is still somewhat out of the ordinary.
毕竟没有旅行者会把自己心爱的行李箱丢在路边——那么久——如果真有，想必那人一定经常丢三落四，或者至少…很有钱。

After all, no traveler would ever throw away their prized luggage on the roadside like that, at least, not for that long. And if this were somehow the case, then the person who left their stuff behind must be habitually forgetful or unbelievably rich.
而且，这位迷糊或富有的旅行者还在自己的提箱里留下了某种不得了的物件。如果伛身侧耳仔细倾听，你便能听到这样一种声音……

Moreover, this forgetful or rich traveler apparently left something quite extraordinary inside this suitcase. If you press your ears against it and have a listen, you can hear a sound that goes like this...
这似乎是某种机械装置读秒的声音。令人沉静，但出现在这里可不太妙——人来人往的车站、会发出滴答声的可疑提箱……

It appears to be some kind of mechanized device knocking a sound with each passing second, calm, soothing. But, wait... Having this thing here is probably problematic. This suspicious suitcase making ticking sounds in a transportation station this busy...
天啊，太可怕了，还是别往下继续说了。呃，你懂这是什么意思吧？相信你一定懂。
Gosh, that's terrible. Stop right there. Uh, you know what that means, right? I'm sure you know.
I know... Call someone, please! There's a bomb here!
停、停，你先别急！会发出这种声音的也未必只有炸弹——它还有可能是块价值连城的金表呢，对不对？在没有得到定论之前，你最好不要引发恐慌。
Shh! Just hold on for a sec! It's not just bombs that could be ticking. What about precious gold watches? You'd better not start a mass panic before a conclusion is reached.
幸运的是，你未经思考的呼喊没有引起任何人的注意；不幸的是，你不知道这箱子里滴答响的东西究竟是炸弹还是金表。
Fortunately, your reckless yelling didn't cause anyone to notice us. And unfortunately, we still don't know if it's a bomb or just some kind of timepiece.
我懂——或许是块昂贵的金表…
I know... Maybe it's a valuable gold watch.
停、停，你先别急！会发出这种声音的也未必只有钟表——它还有可能是颗危险的炸弹呢，对不对？歪脑筋很有可能会要你的命，下次还是别这样了。
Wait, wait, just hold on for a sec! It's not just watches that could be ticking. What about bombs and explosive devices? We'd better be sure of it lest it kills us all.
幸运的是，你的手比大脑更冷静，它暂时还未伸向那诱人的皮箱；不幸的是，你不知道这箱子里滴答响的东西究竟是炸弹还是金表。

Fortunately, you are calmer than what your words suggest, and you haven't reached into that alluring suitcase. And unfortunately, we still don't have a single clue about whether it's a bomb or just some kind of timepiece.
噢，老实说，你的回答十分让人出乎意料。
Well, to be honest, your answer was quite unexpected.
我是想说，这箱子里很有可能揣着一颗炸弹…或是一块价值连城的金表。不过，后者发生的概率比较小…也许。
What I'm trying to say is it could be a bomb in the suitcase... or a precious gold watch. But the possibility for the latter is rather slim... I think.
无论如何，你得打开这个箱子才能知道里面装了什么。但这听起来有点…不道德。
Either way, we've got to open the suitcase to know what is inside. But that sounds a little... dishonest.
换句话说——要想做出这种违反公序良俗的事，你就必须消耗一个「崇高道德的赞许」，以及忍受自己良心的拷问、周围人惊异的目光、银鬃铁卫的通缉…诸如此类。

In other words, to do something that goes against societal ethics would cost you a "Praise of High Morals," not to mention the guilt you'd have to endure, the look you'd get from others, the warrant for your arrest by the Silvermane Guards, and so on.
或者，你就干脆闭上眼、转过身，默默离开这里，当作今天在这里目击到的一切根本不存在——啊，当然还得祈祷那颗可能存在的炸弹不会危害公共安全。

Or, you could just turn a blind eye to all this, turn around, and leave this place, pretending everything you just witnessed has never even happened in the first place. But, of course, you will still have to pray that this potential bomb is not going to risk public safety.
I have to open this suitcase!
没问题！那么，你曾受过「崇高道德的赞许」吗？
No problem! So, were you ever blessed with "Praise of High Morals"?
如果崇高道德确有实体，那么它大概会在此刻向你皱眉。
If High Morals were a real person, it'd probably be frowning so hard at you right now.
但一码归一码——消耗「崇高道德的赞许」后，你就可以心安理得地打开这可疑的箱子了。上吧！
But one thing at a time, you will have nothing to worry about when you consume a "Praise of High Morals" to open this suspicious suitcase. So, go ahead!
都什么时候了还谈这个？
We don't have time for this!
嘿，这是规矩。你懂什么是规矩，我也懂。
Heh, you know them as well as I do that these are the rules.
没有那个镀金的、健美的、昂扬的大拇指人像，你就不能做坏事！明白吗？
Without that proud-looking, gold-plated, thumb-sized bodybuilder figurine, you can't do anything bad! Got it?
Well, then, I will use one.
这就对了——规矩不能坏嘛。消耗「崇高道德的赞许」后，你就可以心安理得地打开这可疑的箱子了。上吧！
That's it. Rules are made to be followed. You will have nothing to worry about when you use a "Praise of High Morals" to open this suspicious suitcase. So, go ahead!
你很急，这可以理解；但擅自打开别人的行李箱就是窥探他人隐私！这是不对的！
I know you are in a hurry. But opening others' suitcases without their explicit permission is an invasion of privacy! This is simply wrong!
你果然是个正直的人。消耗「崇高道德的赞许」后，你就可以心安理得地打开这可疑的箱子了。上吧！
I knew you are a person of integrity. You will have nothing to worry about when you use a "Praise of High Morals" to open this suspicious suitcase. So, go ahead!
喔喔喔——冷静！有什么需求可以坐下来好好谈嘛，别激动！
Whoa whoa whoa, take it easy! Let's just sit down and have a chat. No need to get all riled up.
算了，算了！你请便，就打开它看看吧——反正没人知道这是谁的箱子……
Fine, you go ahead and open it. No one knows whose suitcase this is anyway.
你终于在这空荡荡的手提箱中发现了滴答声的来源——这果然是一只价值连城的金表。
You finally locate the source of that ticking sound in the rather empty suitcase. It turns out to be a precious gold watch!
上面刻有「华劳斯」的字样，另附一行「如有好心人拾到此物，请速联系华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，必有重谢」。

The moniker "Wallace" is engraved on the watch, along with the line, "Should this item come into your possession, kindly contact Wallace Cambridge Limestein. Your act of kindness shall be handsomely recompensed."
看来，这块表会出现在这里是命中注定。这位富豪已经考虑到了所有的可能性了——除了被当成炸弹。

This watch seems destined to be here at this very moment, as its wealthy owner has already made preparations for all the foreseeable fates of this watch, with the possible exception that it might be mistaken for a bomb.
It's still risky. Perhaps I should just leave.
那么，一桩贝洛伯格缆车站的闹剧就此落幕——
Then that will be the end of this farce at Belobog's cable car station.
毕竟…假设它真是一颗炸弹，也不至于就这样迟迟不爆炸，而被丢在这里吃灰了。
If it were a bomb, then why hasn't it ever exploded? All this time, it was just collecting dust.
呃，不管是谁，你踩在我们基地大门上很久了！能让开不？
Um, no matter who you are, can you please just get out of the way? You've been stepping on the door to our base for far too long.
一阵尖锐的叫骂声从下水道中传来。
Angry curses are heard coming from the sewers below.
什么什么声音？我，米歇尔，正和你说话呢！对，就在你脚下！
Wh—What noise? It's me, Michel, speaking to you right now! Yes, right below you!
The manhole cover can talk now!?
什么井盖？！真没素质！我叫米歇尔，懂？
What manhole cover!? How rude! I'm Michel, get it?
哈哈，你还挺有礼貌。我喜欢。
Hah, so you do have manners. I like that.
The manhole cover has a name!?
Do you even understand the words coming out of my mouth?
可惜你把我们基地大门堵死了，要不我非拎着双节棍上去会会你。
You should thank the stars our base's entrance door is now being blocked by you. Otherwise, I would be beating your rear with my nunchucks right about now.
所以，{M#哥们儿}{F#姐们儿}——你能把脚挪开了吗？你要是不走开，我这基地里的警报就停不了，懂吗？
So, {M#bruh}{F#girl}, can you please move your feet away from our door? As long as you're stepping on our door, the alarm in our base won't stop ringing, get it?
哈！可以，你这{M#哥们儿}{F#姐们儿}能处。有机会请你吃太阳薄饼——加奶酪的那种。
Ha! So, this {M#dude}{F#gal} does get it after all. I'll be treating you with a nice suncake, you know, the good kind with cheese and all.
但今天就算了。师傅喊我有事，回见！
But not today, though. Master needs me right now. So, catch you later!
一阵急促的脚步声消失在下水道深处。
Hurried footsteps continue to go further down the sewer until they are completely gone.
我今天就赖在这儿不走了。
What if I just stay put? What can you do about it?
哈！成吧，反正我也拿你没办法。你最好是能在这儿一直赖着，直到我彻底失去耐心为止。
Hah! Fine, it's not like I can force you to do anything. But you'd better be prepared to stay put for a long time until I thoroughly run out of patience.
还好，我提前买了一堆太阳薄饼…咱就看看谁先耗得过谁——喔～双倍奶酪，真香……
Thankfully, I already bought a stack of suncakes... Let's just see who is gonna outlast the other... Mmm, extra cheese, yummy!
下水道里只剩下无穷无尽的吧唧嘴声在回荡。
All that can be heard down the sewer is just munching sounds.
毫无疑问，在你面前的东西是一个衣柜。
Without a doubt, what's in front of you is a closet.
它是一个——仅从你的感官所能接收到的表象而言——完美的衣柜。外观、容量、触感、材质、味道…无论从哪个角度出发，你都想象不出比它更完美的衣柜了。

It is — based on all the signals coming in from your sensory organs — a perfect closet. Design, capacity, tactility, build quality, and scent... No matter how you look at it, you can't possibly think of a better closet.
…等等，味道？味道也是能用来判断衣柜优劣的维度吗？
Wait a minute, scent? How is scent a measurement of how good a closet is?
管它呢，它真好闻。那不是一般的檀木或是任何自然材质的味道…而是完全人造的、源自化学的美妙清香。它真诱人…诱人到你想赶紧钻进衣柜，让这味道缠裹全身。

Doesn't matter. It smells good. And it's not an odor of typical sandalwood or any other natural materials... Rather, it is a miraculously cleansing scent that is completely artificial, a marvel of chemical engineering. It is so alluring that all you want is to get into the closet and let your whole body soak in its scent.
你抵御住了诱惑。现在，最好离这个衣柜远点…化学的气味或许香甜，但对你的健康恐怕没什么益处。
You manage to resist the temptation. And now, you'd better stay away from the closet... As ambrosial as its chemical scent might be, it probably won't do you any good.
Whatever, I'm going in. Here I come, closet!
Yes... you are about to go in!
左脚…右脚…转身…深呼吸…见鬼，为什么要深呼吸？你想打喷嚏，但努力忍住了…站定…然后……
Left foot... Right foot... Turn around... Take a deep breath... Darn it, why a deep breath? You wanna sneeze, but manage to suppress it... Stand firm... And then...
...close the door in one smooth move. Bang!
你成功了——祝贺你，你现在已经与全宇宙最完美的衣柜融为一体了！
Congratulations! You've now been merged with the universe's most perfect closet as one single entity.
不过，老实说…你的心情还远未达到「激动」的境界。或许你还需要点时间才能参会这其中的美妙。
But honestly, your mood is far from reaching the state of "excitement." Perhaps you need more time to comprehend the subtle wonder in all this.
嗯…对于你的视觉感官来说，这地方似乎暗了点儿。不过，你会适应的…对吧？
Hmm... This place might seem a little too dark for you. Guess you will eventually get used to it, right?
哈，就是这股劲！再多等一会儿，再多适应一下这黑暗的环境……
Ha, that's the spirit! Give it more time. Adapt to this dark environment.
出去？你确定？不，我可不建议你那么做。
Go out? Are you sure? No, I don't think that'd be good for you.
相信自己，只要你再稍微坚持一会儿…一定会有比伸手不见五指的黑暗更有趣的事情发生。保准带劲。
Trust yourself. Just wait a little longer... Something more interesting than this pitch darkness will occur. It's guaranteed to be the real deal.
等等，你听到了吗？有个声音正在接近——咔嗒，咔嗒……
Wait, did you hear that? A sound is closing in. Click, click....
你得想办法改变自己消极的人生观了，伙计。不，那才不是人的脚步声，那是——
You've got to change your nihilistic views on life, pal. No, that's not the sound of human footsteps. That's...
不，那才不是什么客房服务…那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
No, that's not room service... That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.
没错，没错！瞧瞧，你已经完全上道了！
Yes, yes! Now you finally get it!
那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.
可不是吗？好好想想，现在——猜猜看，那是谁的脚步声？
That's exactly right! Just think about it. Now, take a guess. Whose footsteps are those?
不，那才不是什么客房服务…那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
No, that's not room service... That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.
没错，没错！瞧瞧，你已经完全上道了！
Yes, yes! Now you finally get it!
那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.
…但这回它挑错了对手！你可是宇宙闻名的开拓者，黑塔空间站的守护神，末日兽的…呃…末日。对——今天就是你为民除害的日子！

It picked the wrong opponent to mess with this time. After all, you are the universally renowned Trailblazer, the guardian deity of the Herta Space Station, the Doomsday Beast's... um... Doom. Yep. Today is the day you purge evil for the greater good!
衣柜里似乎没那么暗了，勇气的光芒照亮了你周围的空间。哇哦。
It doesn't seem to be that dark in the closet. The light of valor illuminates your surroundings. Wow.
Room service... Is anyone in there?
哈，正中下怀！所有的旅馆恶魔都喜欢把自己伪装成「客房服务」。毫无新意。
Ha, there it is! All hotel devils love to disguise themselves as "room service." Such a cliche.
你才不会轻易被它欺骗——你的内心防线比克里珀堆砌的城墙还坚不可摧。你做好准备了。
You won't be easily fooled by it. Your mind is stronger than those city walls built by Qlipoth. You are prepared.
Um... I'm gonna come in now, okay?
时机到了，恶魔——迎接正义的审判吧！
你胜利了！歌德宾馆的恶魔已经被驱逐，这片土地已经得到了净化——
You won! The Goethe Hotel's devil is now banished, and this land is now purified.
呃…那个「恶魔」似乎丢下了什么东西。拿着吧，别客气——就把它当作对你英勇行径的奖赏。
Um... That devil seems to have left something behind. Take it as your reward for your intrepidity.
只是有人在走廊里走动而已。
It's just someone walking in the corridor.
Bang! The closet's door slams open.
黑夜给了我黑色的眼睛…
Dark night gifted me a set of dark eyes...
此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的人物毛色灰白、雌雄莫辨，直视着你的眸子中流露出一股若即若离的生无可恋感。

There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a wanted poster. The figure in the picture is predominantly grayish-white, androgynous, and looking straight at you with a sense of lukewarm lifelessness in their eyes.
再加上那似乎是手的结构连接着一根似乎是球棍的大棒——画面中的人物究竟是谁已不言而喻。
Coupled with a structure that appears to be a hand gripping a baseball bat, it's instantly clear to whom the figure refers.
你决定将它从墙上揭下。为了贝洛伯格，也为了你自己。
You decide to peel it off the wall. For the sake of Belobog — and for your own sake.
此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的人物长得很是别致——两只眼睛一个鼻子一张嘴；如果描述得再深入一点——他的头发是蓝的，眼睛是绿的。

There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The figure is highly unique: Two eyes, a nose, and a mouth; or to be more descriptive, his deep blue hair and jade-green eyes.
这不是在敷衍你，而是画师将人物刻画得太过传神，以至于此处再有任何描述都是画蛇添足。你看，他那似笑非笑的嘲讽神情已经能够说明一切了。
This isn't going over your head, but the portrait really is so expressive that any description here would be gilding the lily. Look, his half-smiling sneer says it all, really.
你决定将它从墙上揭下。主要是为了贝洛伯格——考虑到你在此地经历的种种，你并不是很在乎画中人物的感受。
You decide to peel it off the wall. Mainly for Belobog — thinking of all you've been through here, you don't really care how the character in the poster feels.
此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。不过，画中的人物眉宇间透露着几分沉静，又带有些许威慑，倒不似画作本身那样狂野。
There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. But this guy's eyebrows betray a hint of serenity, as well as slight gravitas — yet not as wild as the poster itself.
你基本上能够确认这位正与你面面相觑的男子是谁了。如果你将画像带回列车交给那人过目，他恐怕会露出与画中完全一致的表情。

You can basically confirm who this guy looking down at you is. If you take the poster back to the train and show it to that person, he'll probably show exactly the same expression as is in the poster.
你决定将它从墙上揭下。为了贝洛伯格，也为了你的同伴。
You decide to peel it off the wall. For the sake of Belobog — and for the sake of your companion.
此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。不得不说，此画艺术造诣之高堪称…骇目惊心。
There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. It has to be said that the aesthetics of this artist are... shocking to the eye.
狂乱的笔触与大胆的配色相结合，将画中人物勾勒得栩栩如生；同时，整幅画面传达出的欢乐情绪简直要溢出纸面，可以说是百分之百地忠于原型了。

The frantic brushwork combines with bold colors to vividly bring the character to life, while the joyful emotion conveyed by the whole portrait spills out from the canvas. You can say it's a hundred percent faithful in its likeness to the original.
你决定将它从墙上揭下。这样的杰作不能公诸于世——至少不能被她看到。
You decide to peel it off the wall. Such a masterpiece can scarcely be made public to the world — in any case, not to her at least.
此处贴着一幅艳美绝俗的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的男性神情真挚，口衔红花，热烈如炽焰。
There's an otherworldly, sensuous portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The guy in the poster appears sincere, gripping a rose in his teeth as he blazes with enthusiasm.
你明白，这就是那种曾风靡一时的某类作品中常见的男一号：永远热情似火，永远主动出击，永远毫不遮掩地散发自己的魅力。我们通常称之为…芳心纵火犯。
You identify him as the classic male lead in a once-popular period genre: Always fiery, always aggressive, always unabashedly charismatic. We usually call this kind of guy... a reckless Lothario.
你决定将它从墙上揭下。毕竟你知道画中人物的原型是谁——你明白，爱是要分对象的。
You decide to peel it off the wall. After all, you know who it's a portrait of — and love must always have an object of its desires.
此处贴着一幅艳美绝俗的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的男性神情淡漠，手执蓝花，冷艳如冰霜。
There's an otherworldly, sensuous portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The guy in the poster appears aloof, holding a blue flower as cool as ice.
你明白，这就是那种曾风靡一时的某类作品中常见的男二号：永远寡淡如水，永远保持距离，永远不遗余力地克制自己的感情。我们通常称之为……

You identify him as the classic second male lead in a once-popular period genre: Always indifferent, always at a distance, always struggling to keep emotions in check. We usually call this kind of guy...
…可恶，你本来想给他取个更有格调的外号。但你的脑子已经被「冷面小青龙」几个字占满了。
...Darn it — you wanted to come up with a cooler nickname. But those kilobytes of brainpower seem preoccupied with an existing name: Cold Dragon Young.
你决定将它从墙上揭下。毕竟你们无名客做好事向来不留名，更遑论如此精致的画像了。
You decide to peel it off the wall. The Nameless have never left behind any credit for good deeds done, let alone for a portrait as exquisite as this.
此处贴着一幅艳美绝俗的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的女性笑靥如花，手捧…呃，相机？含情脉脉地望着你，或者你身后的某处。

There's an otherworldly, sensuous portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The woman in the poster is smiling like a flower, holding a... camera? She looks at you affectionately — or possibly at somewhere just behind you.
老实说，你很难认真看待眼前这精美的人像。它与她是那么相似，却又在各种意义上都很不同——非要说的话，或许是少了一些欢乐…？
Honestly, it's hard to take seriously this beautiful portrait before you. It looks exactly like her, yet different in every sense — if you twisted my arm, perhaps it's a little less joyful...?
你决定将它从墙上揭下。如果她喜欢，再把这通缉令贴回来也不迟。
You decide to peel it off the wall. If she likes it, you can stick it back up.
你能看看这些通缉令吗？
Can you take a look at these wanted posters?
…啊，你竟然把它们全部集齐了。我本来安排了铁卫把它们揭掉，但看来还是你的动作比较快。
...Ah, you got them all together. I asked guards to help me rip them down, but I see that you're far quicker.
你这样也算是帮了我们一个忙。请稍等，我去准备给你的报酬……
You've done me a huge favor. Hang on a moment. Let me prepare a reward for you...
— Hang on. I've something to ask you!
这些…狂野的画像是谁画的？
狂野？你是在说我画的这些吗？
Wild? You mean these paintings I did?
感觉…也没那么狂野吧。可你和佩拉都这么说，难道确实是我画得不好看……
It feels... not that wild at all. But both you and Pela have said that. Am I really a bad painter...
No no, your painting is very vivid!
「传神」…通缉令要的就是这个。能受到你这样高度表扬，我感到很荣幸。谢谢你。
"Vivid"... That's exactly what wanted posters called for. It's an honor to receive such high praise like this from you. Thank you so much.
你看，这幅——我记得你这位同伴是叫「三月七」吧？这张画像是我最满意的…我觉得它非常生动。
Look, this one — I remembered your companion's name is March 7th, right? This is the one I'm proudest of... I think it's indeed very vivid.
没天分就不要学人画画了…
If you don't have the natural talent for it, don't learn how to paint...
…你说得对。画画这方面，我还得多向佩拉请教才是。
...You're right. When it comes to painting, I've still got so much to learn from Pela.
可惜当时情况紧急，画得实在太仓促，否则我还能再打磨一下……
It's a shame that things were so urgent. I had to paint this one in a hurry — otherwise I would've polished it a lot more...
这些…美型的画像是谁画的？
看这华丽的风格…大概是佩拉的作品。原来她还自己重新画了一版啊…这件事我确实不知情。
This gorgeous style... I presume it's Pela's work? So she even painted one version of it herself... I never knew about that.
我只听说她很会画画，还因此有不少拥趸，其他的便不清楚了。如果你实在好奇，可以亲自去问问她相关细节。
All I've heard is that she's a wonderful painter with lots of fans because of it. That's all I know. If you're really curious, you can ask her about the rest in person.
Why are we on this Wanted list?
你忘了吗？当时可可利亚大人称你们意图颠覆贝洛伯格，下令全军通缉你们。情况紧急，我便亲自画了一版通缉令，让部下张贴在各处。

Have you forgotten? Madam Cocolia said you lot were trying to overthrow Belobog, and put out a Wanted order on your heads. It was urgent, so I painted a Wanted poster and got my staff to post them all over the place.
不过，你们已经证明了自己的清白，通缉一事也就到此为止了。先前给你们添了不少麻烦…还得再向你们说声抱歉。
But you've already proved your innocence, and so that's the end of this Wanted matter. I'm sorry for causing you so much trouble before... I owe you another apology.
你能看看这些通缉令吗？
Can you take a look at these wanted posters?
Wanted posters? What are those for?
…这些居然还没撤掉吗？！杰帕…啊不，戍卫官他到底在干什么……
...Haven't these been removed yet!? Gepa— Ack, I mean, what the heck is the captain up to...
啊，我、我…我不认识这些东西，哈哈。什么通缉令？听都没听过。你快拿走吧，想收藏还是想干嘛都随你，再见。
Ah, I—I... I've never seen these before in my life, haha. What Wanted posters? Never heard of 'em. You can take those away. Keep them or do whatever you want. Goodbye.
...Hang on. I've something to ask you!
这些…狂野的画像是谁画的？
不许问其他那些是谁画的。我不知道。你快走吧，我还有事，再见。
Don't ask me who painted the rest. I don't know. Hurry up and move along, I'm busy. Goodbye.
这些…美型的画像是谁画的？
我不知道。画得真好看啊，会是谁呢？好想认识一下哦。
I don't know. They're so beautiful. Who could it be? I'd love to know.
当然啦，我怎么可能知道啊……
Are you sure it's not... YOU!?
好，祝你度过愉快的一天，我先走了……
Well, have a nice day then. I'm off...
…你别再拿那种眼神看我了！我招了，我全都招……
...Don't look at me like that! I confess, I confess to everything...
这些…通缉令…就是我画的啦！这下你满意了吗！
These... Wanted posters... I painted them! Are you happy?
当然满意，画得很好看啊。
Of course I am. They're nice.
求求你别再夸了…我要受不了了……
Please stop it... I can't take any more teasing...
不满意！怎么没给我也画一个？
No way! Why haven't you painted one of me?
没画你是因为…我忘了你长什么样了……
I never painted you because... because I forgot what you looked like...
那个…我都告诉你这么多了，你可千万别跟其他人说啊！这件事你知我知就够了…拜托你了！
Well... I've already said far too much. Please don't tell anyone else! Only us two can know about this... please!
Why are we on this Wanted list?
因为你们意图颠覆贝洛伯格啊，忘了？
Because you tried to overthrow Belobog, remember?
…开个玩笑，我知道你们是清白的。不过，在那之后，杰帕德长官应该已经安排人把通缉令都撤掉了啊——这事还是我当面向他申请的呢。
...Just kidding, I know you're innocent. But after all of that, Captain Gepard should've had your arrest warrants canceled by now — I submitted an application for it in person.
总之…我之后会再去向他确认下状况。要是还有没回收的通缉令就糟了……
Anyway... I'll check in with him later to confirm everything's okay. It'd be terrible if there were any outstanding warrants that weren't revoked...
看来这就是最后一张通缉令了。贝洛伯格的市容市貌因你焕然一新，恭喜你！
This looks like the last one. Belobog's cityscape has been completely revitalized thanks to you. Congratulations!
清点过后，你发现这通缉令统共七张——不过很可惜，即使你费力将这七章画像集齐，也没有被封印的大守护者突然现身，声称能够满足你的任何愿望。
You count and find seven Wanted posters in total — but unfortunately, despite collecting all seven of these, no Supreme Guardian pops out of nowhere to grant fulfillment of your wishes.
有关通缉令的一切到这里就全部结束了。不过，如果你的好奇心足够旺盛，也不妨去找你的铁卫朋友——
That's all there is regarding these Wanted posters. But if you're still curious, you might be able to hit up some of your Silvermane Guard friends —
杰帕德与佩拉——你可以问问他们，这些通缉令为何个个千奇百怪。
Gepard and Pela — you can ask them why these Wanted posters are so strange.
在你眼前的是一个近乎等身的球型仪器。从它布满灰尘的状态可以看出，已经很久没有人用手转动过它了。
In front of you is a spherical device that is almost as tall as a person. The amount of dust on it suggests it has not been used for a while.
「贝洛伯格人都不在乎地理吗？」——这样的疑惑跳入你的脑中。但你仔细回想了一下这颗星球的境遇，发现人们对「地理」这门学科兴致寡淡倒也情有可原。

"Don't Belobogians care about geography?" This question crosses your mind. However, given what this planet has been through, it's not all that surprising that people here have little to no interest in geography.
对贝洛伯格人来说，星球仪只是一件古董玩物。和一张详细标记出所有商铺位置的行政区地图相比，它毫无实用价值。

To Belobogians, this armillary sphere is but an antique plaything. It has no real practical use, especially when compared with a map of the Administrative District that explicitly depicts the locations of all shops.
转动星球仪，复兴地理学！
你情绪高涨，信心满满。动手吧，让地理学再次伟大！
You now feel energized and confident. Give it a go and make geography great again!
太棒了！我最讨厌的就是地理。
Ugh, great. I hate geography the most.
你止住了向星球仪啐上一口的冲动。不文明是其次——主要原因是布洛妮娅就站在你的身后。
You resist the urge to spit on the armillary sphere not just because that'd be rude. Mostly it's because Bronya is standing right behind you.
你花了一段时间在球型的表面仔细搜索，终于找到了贝洛伯格的位置。
You eventually pinpoint Belobog's location on the globe's surface after diligent inspection.
当然，这个古董星球仪上没有直接标识出「贝洛伯格」几个大字——你之所以能找到它的确切位置，全凭此前在列车上看到的雅利洛-Ⅵ全景资料。

Of course, "Belobog" is not inscribed anywhere on the surface of this antique armillary sphere. The only reason you can pinpoint its exact location is that you've once seen Jarilo-VI's panoramic view on the Express.
你的脑中毫无规划，一片混乱。那些杂乱无章的地名被你的视觉神经吸收，下一秒又被干干净净地抛在脑后。

You looked around aimlessly, and all your thoughts are completely muddled together. All those random place names are reflected onto your visual receptors but are immediately discarded by your brain the very next second.
可惜，虽然你的志向远大，但地理就是一门死记硬背的学问。你必须找到一个记忆的基准点，一个突破口……
Despite your great aspirations, geography is a field that relies on memorization. To make a breakthrough, you will have to find an efficient mnemonic.
你花了一段时间在球型的表面仔细搜索，终于找到了贝洛伯格的位置。
You eventually pinpoint Belobog's location on the globe's surface after much effort.
当然，这个古董星球仪上没有直接标识出「贝洛伯格」几个大字——你之所以能找到它的确切位置，全凭此前在列车上看到的雅利洛-Ⅵ全景资料。

Of course, "Belobog" is not inscribed anywhere on the surface of this antique armillary sphere. The only reason you can pinpoint its exact location is that you've once seen Jarilo-VI's panoramic view on the Express.
很好，现在你找到一个靠谱的基准点了。下一步该怎么做？集中思维，控制视线；拨开迷雾，探寻知识。
Now that you finally have a starting point, what should be your next move? Concentrate your thoughts, fine-tune your vision, evict all distractions, and seek truth with your own eyes.
东、南、西、北——你总要先定下一个「开拓」的方向。
North, east, south, and west. You must first decide on the next trailblazing direction.
你看向东方。那是一片广袤的内陆平原，被错综复杂的边境线划分成数十个区块。每个区块可能代表着一个城邦，甚至是一个国家。
You look to the east and see a vast inland plain that is partitioned into dozens of subdivisions by a labyrinthine web of borders. Each subdivision could be a city or even a whole country.
你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你仿佛看到了鳞次栉比的地髓工厂，来自各个世界的商人、游客在城市的街巷间游荡，诉说着令人啧啧称奇的旅行故事。

Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you see orderly lined arrays of Geomarrow factories, merchants coming from various worlds, and tourists lingering in the city streets while sharing fascinating tales about their travels.
内陆平原的最东边零散分布着几个小城镇，它们的名字都无比拗口。除此以外，你没有发现什么有趣的东西。
Situated at the farthest east of the inland plain are some tiny settlements with names that are hard to pronounce. Other than this, you fail to discover anything noteworthy.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
内陆的东北方向是一片空白之地。或许那里土地荒芜，不宜居住？无论如何，有关它的秘密都已经被历史埋葬。
What can be found in the northeast are nothing but barren lands. Perhaps those lands are just too desolate to be habitable? Whatever the case might be, its secrets remain deeply buried in history.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
内陆平原的东南方是一个被峡谷隔开的孤单国度，它的名字已经难以看清。灾难降临时，那里的人们一定绝望而无助。
Laying in the southeast of the inland plain is a deserted realm isolated by canyons. The name is no longer legible. When the calamity fell, its people must have been devastated.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
你看向西方。那里是无垠的大海，尚未被寒潮永久冰封。
You look to the west and see a great ocean that is yet to be permanently encased in ice by the Eternal Freeze.
你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你好像看到了浩瀚的蓝洋，造型各异的船只舰艇浮游在粼粼波光之上。
Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you seem to catch sight of a blue ocean with vessels of every shape sailing on its dazzling waves.
西之西，无穷无尽的海洋。不知道这个世界的原住民用了多长时间才发现自己的家园是一颗球体。
The furthest west is an endless ocean. It's not known how long it took the inhabitants of this world to discover that their home is situated on a globe.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
海洋上零星散布着几个没有被命名的小岛。也许这个世界也曾有流浪汉留下孤岛求生的日记？
Scattered in the ocean are a few unnamed isles. Perhaps there are journals left behind by drifters documenting their survival on those isolated islands?
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
往海洋的西南方看去，你发现一片巨大的黑色圆形。它一度象征着死亡和恐怖，漩涡会无情地吞没一切船只和生命。令人着迷。
You look to the southwest of the ocean and spot a colossal black circle. It once represented death and terror, a vortex engulfing all ships and lives. Truly fascinating.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
你看向南方。那是一片巨大的盆地，整个区块被繁多的森林、湖泊和湿地点缀。
You look to the south and see a giant basin, where the whole region is decorated by many forests, lakes, and wetlands.
你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你好像看到了自然焕发的盎然生机，形态各异的动物在天空、陆地和水中迸发着蓬勃的生命力。

Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you seem to witness nature's utmost exuberance, with animals of every shape thriving in the air, on the land, and in the water.
一片淡绿色的湖泊，那里的气候一定十分温暖，并且栖息着很多奇特的生物——比如翼展两米的蜻蜓，数十吨重的巨鳄，还有……
A greenish lake with a warm climate. Many marvelous species choose to reside here, from dragonflies with wings that span six feet to giant crocodiles that weigh dozens of tons, and many besides...
呃，别想太多。或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Um. Don't make too much of it. Perhaps you should just change direction and explore again.
一大片森林——大到难以置信，大到几乎占有和海洋近似的面积。肯定有不少反物质军团的兵卒在那里迷了路。
It's a forest so unbelievably large that it could almost match the size of an ocean. There must've been a great many soldiers of the Antimatter Legion who got lost in it.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
沙漠…紧邻着森林和湖区，真是神奇的地理地貌。沙漠里也散布着几座带有名字的城市，养活它们的可能是地下丰富的地髓资源促进的贸易。

A desert very close to lakes and forests... What a peculiar landscape. There are a few named cities scattered in the desert. What sustains them must've been the trade fueled by the rich Geomarrow resources found underground.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
你看向北方。那是一大片耸立的山脉，一直蔓延向世界的尽头。
You look to the north and see stunningly high mountain ranges that extend all the way to the world's end.
你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你仿佛看到了高耸的山峰插入云霄，从尚未被白雪覆盖的山崖向远处眺望，好一番恢弘壮阔的景象。

Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you seem to catch a glimpse of cloud-piercing summits and a breathtaking view on top of a mountain ridge that is yet to be covered by snow.
北岳之北，是一座名叫查拉图斯特拉萨帕拉——你放弃了读出那个名字——的山峰。毋庸说，那应该就是雅利洛-Ⅵ的最高峰。
North of the northern mountains is a summit called Zarathustrazapala, the name of which you've already given up trying to pronounce. That is indubitably Jarilo-VI's highest point.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
Northwest... Wait a minute, this is...
你的视线停在一片被群山包围的地块上。它或许是平平无奇的洼地，又或许是什么雅利洛-Ⅵ独有的奇特地貌…但对你来说，它究竟是什么并不重要。

You set eyes upon a land surrounded by mountain ranges. It might just be a typical marshland, or perhaps some peculiar landscape exclusive to Jarilo-VI... But that makes little to no difference to you either way.
唯一重要的一点是：那片地块在星球仪上呈现出来的形状，简直和列车长帕姆一模一样！
Perhaps the only meaningful aspect is how that piece of land on the armillary sphere is shaped exactly like Conductor Pom-Pom.
你不禁展开了联想——也许「开拓」阿基维利上次来到这颗星球时过于流连忘返，于是在临走前给雅利洛-Ⅵ盖下了印章……
You can't help but think it might be a commemorative stamp left by Akivili the Trailblaze when he last paid a visit to this planet...
真是个奇妙的发现。看吧，枯燥如地理学也偶有其美妙之处。
What a fascinating discovery. Look, there are jewels of wonder even in a seemingly dull discipline like geography.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
群山，群山，还是群山。你几乎看到了军团来犯之后，无助的灾民躲入山间，等待末日来临的凄惨景象。

Mountains, mountains, and even more mountains. You can almost see how miserable it must've been for all those refugees who fled into the mountains, seeking shelter after the Legion's attack while helplessly waiting for calamity's arrival.
或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.
你结束了这段穿越千年的地理之旅。星球仪静静地停在你面前，原本布满灰尘的表面多出了几个显眼的巴掌印。
You end this geographical journey spanning millennia. The armillary sphere comes to a quiet stop in front of you. There are now a few discernible handprints left on its previously dusty surface.
Now, it's time for an exam.
——开玩笑的，没有人不痛恨应试教育。就让和古代雅利洛-Ⅵ有关的地理知识停留在兴趣的范畴吧。
Just kidding. Everyone despises those good-for-nothing exams. Let's just keep the study of Jarilo-VI's ancient geography confined within the realm of extracurricular pursuits.
过去的荣誉，已经无法评判我现在的成就，区区第一真狸大学的一等荣誉学位，在我看来已经不值一提。
My past honors are no longer a measure of my current achievements. A mere first-class honors degree from University of Furberitas Prime is, in my opinion, hardly worth mentioning.
既然来了二相乐园，这回我真狸医生要去绘世学院进修人生中的第九个博士学位。
Now that I'm in Planarcadia, I, Dr. Furatio, shall be heading to Graphia Academy to pursue my ninth doctorate.
拉帝奥教授，有人冒充你。
习惯了，经常有人冒充我的名字发表一些庸俗、愚钝、可笑至极的观点…不对，你什么意思哩？
I'm used to it. People are always using my name to publish vulgar, dull, and utterly ridiculous opinions... Wait, what do you mean by that?
绘世学院发博士学位吗？
绘世学院，那不是直接发院士学位吗？
Graphia Academy? Don't they just give you the academician title on the spot?
什、什么！那好吧，我对学术已经厌倦哩！这回我要读的是一个博升专学位——算了，编不下去哩！
Wh—What? Fine, then! I've grown tired of academia anyway! This time, I shall pursue a post-doctoral degree... Ugh, who am I kidding... I can't keep this up!
天才的头脑需要辅之以必要的艺术修养，俗话说，不会画画的物理学家不是好的数学老师。另外，注意你言语里的错别字…不对，你是不是发现什么哩？

A brilliant mind must be complemented by the necessary artistic refinement. As they say, a physicist who cannot paint will not make a good mathematics teacher. Also, "fantastic" spells with a "c"... Wait a minute, did you do that on purpose?
嘶…你是什么时候看出我不是本尊的？不管怎么说，千变万化本来就是狸狸的生存之道，狸狸的事你不要多管！
Hmph... When did you realize I wasn't him? Regardless, constant transformation is the furbo way of life. You stay out of my business!
狸狸错了…敢问大人何方神圣？
Sorry about that... May I inquire who I'm speaking with?
银河球棒侠？这个名字是不是在哪里……
Galactic Baseballer? Where have I heard that name before?
…啊！您、您难道就是我们《狸狸周刊》传说中的新老板？
Ah! A—Are you the new owner of our Furbobo Weekly?
我不信。这肯定是你自封的！到底…你到底是什么来头？
I don't buy it. You must have given yourself that title! Just who... who in the world are you?
In that case, check out my name card...
The new boss of furbo Magazines!
啊？哈哈哈哈，大人您真会开玩……
Huh? Hahaha, you really know your way of joking around...
The new boss of furbo Magazines.
啊？哈哈哈哈，大人您真会开玩……
Huh? Hahaha, you really know your way of joking around...
啊啊啊，不要哇，在下只是一时糊涂，变作拉帝奥教授的模样，想享受几句对智者的赞美哩，在下真的错了啊啊啊……

Aaaaah, please nooo! This humble one merely had a momentary lapse in judgment, taking on Professor Ratio's form just to bask in a few words of praise meant for the wise! This humble one is truly sorry, aaaaaah...
I'll be merciful, if only to gain furbo allegiance.
在下再也不敢了，在下保证以后永远不动歪脑筋……在下这就去正经工作，老板再见！
I wouldn't dare do it again, I promise I'll never have any funny ideas... I'll go do some honest work right now, farewell boss!
哼，小气的老板！再见——再也不见！
Hmph, what a petty boss! Goodbye, and good riddance!
在你眼前的宽阔的办公桌属于这个星球的最高统治者——贝洛伯格的大守护者。它看起来尊贵、典雅，被打理得井井有条，与它的主人如出一辙。
The wide desk before you belongs to the majestic ruler of this planet — the Supreme Guardian of Belobog. It looks distinguished, elegant, and well-kept — just like its owner.
当看到这样一张办公桌时，不同的人会有不同反应：热爱秩序的人视之为完美的艺术品，啧啧称奇后便转身离去……
Different people may have different reactions when faced with such a desk: Those who love order view it as a perfect masterpiece, and leave filled with admiration...
…而钟情于混乱的人则开始摩拳擦掌、试图让它回归宇宙无尽熵增的铁律中，并很快给自己找好了由头——「翻箱倒柜是勇者的特权」。

...While those who love chaos lick their lips as they try to return things to the lawless entropy of the universe, quickly finding reason enough for themselves: "The valiant reserve the right to rummage through this desk."
那么，你认为自己是哪一种人呢？
So then, which kind of person are you?
自从被那位负创之神瞥视过后，你的内心深处就常有这样的念头了。这种想法本身无可厚非——只是，雅利洛-Ⅵ已成型的道德体系暂时不大认可这种行为。
You've felt this way ever since you were first glimpsed by the Blemished One. It's an understandable idea — it's just that this morality system of Jarilo-VI doesn't approve of this kind of behavior.
换言之，想在大守护者的办公桌上大闹一番是要付出代价的……
In other words, there's a price to pay for causing trouble at the Supreme Guardian's desk...
好在你在旅途中一路积善行德，曾收获不少来自道德层面的赞许。这点小小的缺德事怎会让你困扰？显然不会。
Luckily, you've built up a record of good deeds and praise along the way. Surely this little slip-up won't bother you? Obviously not.
退一万步讲，这里搞不好藏着珍贵的道具呢！你可千万不能错过——成功将自己说服，你便开始了自己的「工作」……
Take one big step back here — there must be something precious hidden here! You can't miss out on whatever it is. Having convinced yourself, you begin with your "work"...
想想看，当别人知道这件事后，会如何评价你和一众开拓客？——想到这里，你便觉得实在良心难安，于是悻悻然放弃了搞破坏的想法。
Think about it: What will people think of you and the Trailblazers once they find out about this? This thought leaves you feeling deeply uneasy, and so you give up this dastardly plan of yours.
你拒绝释放内心深处那无处发泄的破坏欲，只给办公桌留下了一个潇洒的背影。
You refuse to unleash your pent-up destructive desires, instead turning away to show your best side.
看看这里有些什么东西…
Let's see what's in here then...
首先是一盆可爱的花卉造景。你问为什么是造景？这是因为你看它常年摆放在此处，始终未有凋落的迹象…就像一朵真的假花。
First up is a cute flowerpot scene here. Why is it a scene? Because you've seen this year-round, and it's never wilted of lost leaves before... Just like a fake plant.
为了确认这一想法究竟是否正确，你试着掐了掐那柔嫩的花瓣——
To confirm the veracity of this hunch, you try to pinch one of the tender petals...
啊哦，出汁了。好吧，它不是造景。对不起。
Oh, wow — some sap. Alright, it's not fake. I'm sorry.
再看看有没有其他好玩的…
Let's see if there's anything else fun...
桌上放着几册装帧精美的书籍。你趁没人注意，便将其翻开随意浏览了起来——
Several beautifully bound books are on the desk. When nobody's looking, you crack one of them and browse through at random...
空的、空的、还是空的。难道这些书籍只是为了显示书桌主人高贵教养的装饰品吗？
Empty, empty, and still empty. Could these books merely be ornaments to show off the classy upbringing of their owner?
你还以为这里会有克里珀堡的机密文件呢。真没劲。至少得给本阅读物吧？
You really thought there'll be some top-secret documents on Qlipoth Fort here. How boring. At least give me something to read?
那就让我看看柜子里有什么…
Then let's take a look at what's in the closet...
柜子里肯定有东西——或者说，必须得有。不然你费尽心思翻箱倒柜是为了什么？总不可能真是为了制造破坏吧？
There's definitely something in the closet — or rather, there must be. Otherwise, why are you spending so much energy rummaging through these closets? It can't be to cause actual damage, right?
于是，你将手伸向了那仿佛掩藏了贝洛伯格一切秘密的柜门……
So, you reach out to the handle of the closet that seemingly contains every single secret in all of Belobog...
呃，{NICKNAME}，请你停手吧…这里真没东西，我保证。
Uh, {NICKNAME}, please stop that... There's nothing here, I promise you.
你直起腰，与布洛妮娅四目相对。
You lift your head and stare straight into Bronya's eyes.
这下糟了——她一定是早就发现你在这里鬼鬼祟祟，出于情面才没有立即制止你。怪不得你总觉得附近有道凌厉的目光呢……
This is bad — she must have found out you were snooping around here ages ago, but didn't stop you because she cared. No wonder you always feel as though a burning gaze is affixed upon you...
今天的事，我就睁一只眼闭一只眼…但希望你下次不要这样了。
I'll turn a blind eye today... but I hope that you won't do this next time.
你放心，情况没有你想象得那么夸张。
Don't worry. It's not as bad as you think.
还是惩罚我吧，布洛妮娅大人！
…你怎么这么积极啊？放心，情况没有你想象得那么夸张。
...No need to make it weird. Don't worry. It's not as bad as you think.
当然了，毕竟这书桌只是个装饰。
Of course, this desk is just for decoration.
其实这里只用作接待访客，我的工作场所另在别处。这是出于安全考虑——
This room's actually just where I receive guests, and my workspace is elsewhere. For the sake of security...
毕竟…想要翻大守护者办公桌的人可不止你一个呢。如果换作别人，现在恐怕已经被铁卫押走了吧。
After all... You're not the only one trying to rummage through the Supreme Guardian's desk. If it were anyone else, they'd be apprehended by the Silvermane Guards by now.
噗…别一本正经地道歉了，今天这事就当没发生过吧。
Pfft... Don't be so serious. Let's pretend that this never happened today.
可我什么也没得到，好亏！
But I got nothing, What a lousy bargain!
噗…这话倒是出乎我的意料。
Pfft... Now I didn't expect to hear that.
啊，对了。正好你在这儿——我有样东西要给你。
That's right. It's good that you're here — I've got something for you.
这是「科技护具」的配方，据说是寒潮之前的天外来客带来的技术…可惜，我们现在拥有的资源和技术还暂时无法将其复现。

This is the recipe for High-Tech Protective Gear, rumored to be brought in by visitors from beyond the sky before the Eternal Freeze... Unfortunately, with the resources and technology we have access to, we have yet to be able to reproduce it.
我想你在旅途中总会身陷险境，这护具或许能为你提供防护…你就尽管拿去用吧，别客气。
You're always in danger during your travels, and this protective gear might just protect you... Just use it. Don't be hesitant about it.
广场上的一眼喷泉，泉水凉爽而不寒冷。透过粼粼水波，你看见喷泉底部不规则地铺着一层金属钱币，想来这里也被当地人派上了许愿池的用场。

A fountain in the plaza is shooting out cool, tepid water. You can see through the ripples that the bottom of the fountain has metal coins strewn around. It looks like the locals use this as a kind of wishing pool.
Count how much money there is.
你穷极无聊，决定数数喷泉里有多少钱——用无聊这个词，可能都是一种抬举——要知道喷泉里什么怪东西都有，硬币更是数不胜数。

You're so bored that you decide to count the money on the floor of the fountain — and "bored" is putting it lightly. There are loads of weird stuff floating down there, and there are more coins than can be counted.
你得用点更科学的办法才行。
You'll need a more scientific method.
明白，最简单的办法往往最实用。泉水瀑落，反射的光线刺激着你的眼睛，直到所有东西在你看来都产生了三个重影……
You understand that the simplest solutions are often the most practical. As the waters fall, light refracts into your eyes until three superimposed images form before you...
你以五为单位，每数五个就停下来。不得不说，这一招最初卓有成效，但随着时间的推移，你的眼睛开始疲劳，记忆也不那么靠谱了……
You break things down into groups of five. This trick works great at first, but over time your eyes get tired and your memory grows unreliable...
并不是叫科学计数法的办法就很科学，你懂得这个道理，但还是决定给自己加点挑战。就这样，数字在你的脑中颠来倒去，妙谛无穷……

It's not that scientific notation is scientific per se. You understand this but still decide to give yourself a little challenge. Just like that, the numbers tumble through your brain with their limitless, profound meaning...
试试计算机图形学原理…？
Try utilizing principles of computer graphics...?
你眼睛一眨，已经将整个喷泉的模样化作高分辨率的图片保存在脑中；接着，你将这副图片在脑中二值化，利用形态学技术开始了疯狂运算……
In a flash, you transform the entire fountain into a hi-res image and save it in your memory. Next, you binarize these images in your mind, and use morphology tech to begin your crazy calculation...
最终，你还是数出了喷泉里的硬币数量：1{TEXTJOIN#44}{TEXTJOIN#45}。这个数字有意义吗？可能有。你把这个数字记了下来，但之后数字或许将是另一个数字了。
In the end, you reach the final count: 1{TEXTJOIN#44}{TEXTJOIN#45} coins. Is this number pertinent? Perhaps. You write it down, but then again the number might change later on.
Toss in a coin and make a wish.
你放弃了许愿。就这样吧，喷泉欢迎你之后再来拜访。
You've given up on wishes. That's it. You're welcome to visit the fountain again.
你摸出一枚本地硬币，掷入空中，同时心中默默许愿。愿望许完，硬币已经沉入泉水，成为无数反光物中平平无奇的一员了。
You pull out a local coin and make a wish as it flips through the air. Afterwards, the coin sinks down into the water, becoming just another unremarkable reflection among all the others.
有那么一会儿，什么都没有发生，你还以为自己受了欺骗。但——瞧！硬币落下就像第一块骨牌的失衡，引发了一系列连锁效应：有什么东西悠悠浮了上来，你赶紧抓住它。
For a while, nothing happens and you begin to feel deceived. But — look! The coin tumbles like the first domino, triggering a cascade of effects: Something floats on up, and you grab it.
你耐心等着愿望实现，却听见身后传来一声厉喝——原来这里禁止投币。你赶紧转身走开，也无暇思考愿望到底成真了没有。

You patiently wait for your wish to come true, when you hear a loud shout from behind — It turns out that you're not allowed to toss coins here. You hurriedly turn to walk away, with no time left to wonder if your wish came true.
你耐心等着愿望实现。长长的时间过去了，什么都没有发生，你得到的只有虚无。
You patiently wait for your wish to come true. A long time passes but nothing at all happens, and ends up with a fistful of nothingness.
Fish the treasure out of the fountain.
…等下，你认真的？这可是在广场上，大庭广众众目睽睽之下…退一万步说，就算没有人看见，它可是公共设施啊！这么做你的良心不会痛吗？

...Hang on, are you serious? This is the plaza, in full view of the public... Take one big step back here: Even if nobody saw you, it's a public amenity! Wouldn't your conscience prevent you from doing this?
罢了罢了，看在这个想法十分具有创意的份上——尽管放手去做吧！只是…后果自负。
Forget it, seeing as this is such a creative idea — just go ahead and do it! But do so... at your own risk.
不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，和远古帝王的宝藏相比也毫不逊色！看！你居然从喷泉里找到了——固态净水！？在水池里找到这种东西，真是太合理了！

It begs saying that this fountain really does have everything in it, just like the ancient treasure tombs of the emperors gone by! Look! You somehow find amidst those ripples — solid water!? Makes sense to find it inside a wishing well, I suppose!
不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，和远古帝王的宝藏相比也毫不逊色！看！你居然从喷泉里找到了——吉姆•罗杰汽水？真是暴殄天物！

It begs to say that this fountain really does have everything in it, just like the ancient treasure tombs of emperors in the past! Look! You somehow find amidst those ripples — Jim Roger bread soda!? What a waste!
不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，和远古帝王的宝藏相比也毫不逊色！看！你居然从喷泉里找到了——熄灭原核？！难道反物质军团也在这里许过愿吗？

It begs to say that this fountain really does have everything in it, just like the ancient treasure tombs of the emperors gone by! Look! You somehow find amidst those ripples — Extinguished Core!? Has the Antimatter Legion ever made a wish here!?
你跳进喷泉，却发现今天的喷泉囊中羞涩，并没有什么有趣的东西。但你就这么湿漉漉地空手而归，总觉得有点吃亏。于是你顺手拿走了三块垃圾。

You jump into the fountain, only to find that there's nothing interesting there today. But you can't go back empty-handed and bedraggled like this, so you pick up three pieces of trash on your way out.
不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，当然也包括硬币。你就是来干这个的——一枚脏兮兮的硬币——你最珍贵的宝藏！
It begs saying that this fountain really does have everything in it, including coins of course. That's what you're here for — one filthy coin, your most precious of treasures!
喷泉还是那个喷泉，或者说，许愿池。其中有价值的物件已被你一扫而空，只剩下水中层层叠叠的锈蚀硬币。
The fountain is still the same old fountain, or rather a wishing well. You've already swept away anything valuable in it, leaving behind only stacks of rusted coins.
你已经捞不到任何东西了。但你仍可以凭着兴趣数数它们到底有多少，或者投掷硬币许个愿——如果你还有硬币的话。
There's nothing left for you to get. But you can still count everything out of interest, or toss a coin in to make a wish — if you have any coins left, that is.
你放弃了许愿。就这样吧，喷泉欢迎你之后再来拜访。
You've given up on wishes. That's it. You're welcome to visit the fountain again.
你摸出一枚本地硬币，掷入空中，同时心中默默许愿。愿望许完，硬币已经沉入泉水，成为无数反光物中平平无奇的一员了。
You pull out a local coin and make a wish as it flips through the air. Afterwards, the coin sinks down into the water, becoming just another unremarkable reflection among all the others.
直觉告诉你，许愿池似乎已经无法为你提供更多东西了。
Intuition tells you that the fountain cannot provide you with anything else.
谢天谢地，这什么逻辑语言中枢可算是卖出去了……
Thanks the heavens, this language module thing is finally sold...
我还以为这是没人要的垃圾哩。总之，多谢老板！
I just thought this is trash that nobody wanted. Anyway, thanks very much, boss!
我找到了逻辑语言中枢…
哇，这就是我一直在找的零件！只要有了这个……
Wow, this is just the part I've been looking for! As long as I have this...
那个，虽然这么说有点不合适…但你可以把它让给我吗？求你了，它对我来说真的很重要！
Um, it's awkward to say, but... Could you possibly let me have that? Please, it really would mean a lot to me!
…我想也是，那你出个价吧！我尽量凑钱给你……
...That makes sense. Name your price, then! I'll do my best to put some money together for you...
…谢谢，你真好！那我就先收下了。这元件肯定能派上用场。
...Thank you! You're so kind! Then I'll hold onto that for you. This component will definitely come in handy.
不过，既然要打造能够和人进行「情感互动」的机器人，光是有这样一个模块还是不够的……
But if we want to build an emotionally interactive robot, a simple module like this won't be enough...
——没错，我们还得把「情感」写入其中才行！要是你们有时间了，请一定要来帮帮我呀。
...That's right, we have to code emotion into it! If you have the time, please come and give me a hand.
您好，{NICKNAME}。我正在收集可用于「情感互动」的数据集。
Hello, {NICKNAME}. I am gathering datasets that can be used for emotional interactivity.
我的主人菲多拉称您能为我提供合适的数据集——在学习过程中，我会为您随机预设一社交情景，您只需根据经验作答即可。

My master Fidora said that you can provide me with a suitable dataset. During the learning process, I will randomly present you with a social interaction. Please answer based on your personal experiences.
您输入的对话将被记录，并被逐字解析。
Your conversation will be recorded and parsed word-by-word.
Things are starting to get a little tense...
即将正式进入学习环节。您准备好了吗？
Initiating learning phase: Are you ready?
您可以随时回到这里协助训练。再见！
You can come back here any time to practice. Goodbye!
菲多拉最近心情很好，她邀请我去搏击俱乐部观看比赛。
Fidora has been in high spirits lately, and she invites me to watch a bout at the Fight Club.
在菲多拉支持的拳手挥出致胜一拳后，她看向我，并期待着我说些什么。
After the winning punch from the boxer Fidora is backing, she looks at me in anticipation of me saying something.
作为她的朋友，我此时的回复应该是？
As her friend, what should my reply be at this time?
再描述一下对话情景吧。
Describe the scenario for me once more.
What an amazing punch that was!
Is that all you need to become a fighter?
你的饮料洒在我铁皮上了。
You spilled some drink on my chrome.
学习完成。即将进入下一个场景。
菲多拉最近学会了一首新歌。通过音频比对，我发现她的歌声与原曲差异很大。
Fidora has recently learned a new song. Through comparative audio analysis, I've discovered that her vocals are very different from the original.
即便如此，菲多拉总是不自觉地哼唱起这首歌，突然有一天，她问我自己唱得是否好听。
Even so, Fidora always hums along to this song involuntarily. One day, she suddenly asks me whether her singing is good or not.
作为她的朋友，请问我这个时候该说什么？
As her friend, what should I say at such a time?
It's brilliant. Can you teach me?
That's the extent of your singing?
检测到超过正常水平的噪音。
菲多拉最近发现，巴列维在卖几件从未见过的古代遗物，价格昂贵。
Fidora has recently discovered that Balaway is selling several never-before-seen ancient relics at astronomical prices.
菲多拉既想买下来，又想省钱。纠结之际，她希望我能出出主意。
Fidora wants to buy things, but also wants to save money. At this frustrating juncture, she hopes you can come up with an idea.
作为她的朋友，请问我这个时候该说什么？
As her friend, what should I say at such a time?
我可以为你分担一部分！
I can split it with you!
哈，你钱包里还剩几个子儿？
Save it. How much do you have left in your purse?
你按照自己的风格，帮助它完成了大量情景对话……
Using your own style, you help it get through a ton of scenarios...
感谢你！它学到了不少临场反应，说不定真的能和人正常交流呢。
Thank you! It learned lots of on-the-spot reactions. Perhaps it really can communicate with people normally.
结果到底会怎么样呢…好期待啊！
How is everything going to turn out... I can't wait to see!
训练模式已结束，请稍后再试。
Training module finished. Please try again later.
我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，确实进步飞快。
My robot improved in leaps and bounds after learning how to speak from you.
它的说话方式…该怎么说呢？有点热情却又不失克制，遇到问题总会第一时间为我考虑…如果要交朋友，这种性格应该就是不二之选了吧！

The way it speaks... How to put it? A little enthusiastic, but restrained. Always putting me first whenever we run into any problem... If I were making new friends, this would be the kind of personality I'd want!
总之，它的训练结果十分完美——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
All in all, its training went perfectly — thank you so much, {NICKNAME}!
不过，它的说话的方式实在是…太令人尴尬了，而且还总喜欢抬杠……
However, the way it speaks is kind of... awkward, and it loves to argue...
它总是自信满满地讲一些伤人的话…一两次我还能当它开玩笑，但是太过频繁了就……
It's always saying these cruel jokes at my expense... Once or twice would be okay, but it's just too much...
You don't seem very happy about it.
也许是我自己的问题，但我真的不太能接受……
Perhaps it's just me, but I can't really take it...
哎，不管怎么样，它的训练也算上有所成果——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
Ah, its training has paid off in any case — thank you, {NICKNAME}!
我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，似乎毫无进步……
My robot didn't seem to make any improvements after learning how to speak from you...
它的说话方式还是那么生硬，完全不懂得变通，没有一点儿正常社交的意思。
It still speaks so bluntly, doesn't know how to adapt at all, and doesn't convey any real sense of social interaction.
这…不得从互相了解开始吗？
Shouldn't... things start out by getting to know each other first?
那也得它有这个意愿才行啊……
Then it has to have the will to do so...
我只是在怀疑，他是不是什么都没学到？难道是零件出故障了？又也许是学习的样本数不够……
I was just wondering, did it not learn anything? Was that a mechanical failure? Or maybe it didn't study enough samples...
哎，不管怎么样，它的训练也算上有所成果——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
Ah, its training has paid off in any case — thank you, {NICKNAME}!
我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，确实进步飞快……
My robot improved in leaps and bounds after learning how to speak from you...
它的说话方式该怎么说呢…有点让人琢磨不透。
The way it speaks is just... a little cryptic.
它的言谈举止非常分裂，有时候盛气凌人，有时又特别温柔，偶尔还会对我不理不睬……
It's very disjointed when it speaks, sometimes domineering but sometimes gentle — and sometimes even ignoring me...
Haven't those been an abundant variety of experiences?
我觉得可能是底层系统出现了问题，导致它有了不稳定的倾向。
I think there may be a problem with the core systems causing it to lose stability.
不过作为朋友，能保持一定的新鲜感倒也不是什么坏事……
But as a friend, it's not always a bad thing to have such fresh opinions...
现在比较流行这种随机感。
Being spontaneous is totally in right now.
哎，或许你说得对。总之——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
Hey, maybe you're right. Anyway, thank you, {NICKNAME}!
你好，我是菲多拉的「情感互动」机器人，请多关照！
Hello, I'm Fidora's emotional interactive robot. Nice to meet you!
看什么看，没见过机器人啊？
What are you looking at? Have you never seen a robot before?
系统提示：你正处在本机预设的行进路线上，请及时避让，以免发生危险。
System prompt: You are standing on an intended travel pathway. Please step aside to avoid any harm.
看什么看？█ █ ██错误，错误█ ██ █你好，请多关照！
What are you looking at? █ █ ██ Error, error █ ██ █ Hello, nice to meet you!
我来取星轨之兔的周边…
I'm here to pick up the Star Rabbit merch...
哈哈，我就知道你感兴趣。认真参与「星轨之兔」的银河玩家，请看，这就是客人为您的游戏热情献上的特别纪念——
Haha, I knew you'd be interested. Attention, dedicated cosmic players of "Star Rabbit!" Behold, a special commemorative gift from our guest to celebrate your gaming passion!
显而易见，这上面画了一只毛茸茸的右手。很可能来自一只布偶，或者……
It's obviously a drawing of a furry right hand. It probably belongs to a stuffed toy, or...
这我就不知道啦。那位神秘的客人只是说，「星轨之兔」是一款纪念烬土纪元无名客们与这颗星球并肩作战的游戏。
Beats me. That mysterious guest just said that "Star Rabbit" is a game commemorating the Nameless who fought alongside this planet during the Cindearth Age.
设计方根据当年那些无名客留下的只言片语，在现实中投放了可以帮助大家通关游戏的尘灵助手，也用这种方式向他们表达敬意。

Based on the fragmented accounts left behind by those Nameless long ago, the developers released wispae helpers in the real world to assist everyone in beating the game. A tribute to those trailblazers, in their own way.
客人说，一位现代的无名客也在游玩「星轨之兔」，这让他们又惊又喜。他们决定为您准备一套特殊的礼品。
The guest says that a modern-day Nameless is also enjoying "Star Rabbit," which delights and surprises them. They've decided to prepare a special gift set for you.
现在您收到的只是这套礼品的第一份——随着体验「星轨之兔」的关卡越来越多，您还会收到更多类似的礼品；如果在未来能够全部收齐，或许还会有意想不到的好事发生。

What you've received is just the first piece of this gift set. As you complete more Star Rabbit stages, you'll receive additional pieces. If you manage to collect them all, something unexpectedly wonderful might just happen.
看来又是一场收集之旅！
哈哈。看到您这么有兴致，那位客人应该也很开心吧。
Haha. With you showing this much enthusiasm, I bet that guest is over the moon right now.
对了对了，这里还有客人留下的一封信，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
Oh, oh! By the way, a guest left you a letter. Feel free to take a look when you get a chance. I'll go ahead and get back to what I was doing!
...all this sneaking around and hiding stuff, it's really killing the vibe.
我们的这位客人比较害羞啦。如果时机成熟，TA肯定还是想和您见一面的。
Our guest here is a bit on the shy side. But when the time's right, they'll definitely want to meet you in person.
对了对了，这里还有客人留下的一封信，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
Oh, oh! By the way, a guest left you a letter. Feel free to take a look when you get a chance. I'll go ahead and get back to what I was doing!
Is this commemorative card set worth anything of value?
不清楚呢。不过，在轮值酒保这里寄存私人物品可不是免费的…只要您还没有收到，那位客人每天都要向世界尽头酒馆支付<unbreak>3000</unbreak>信用点和一个原创笑话：代价还是蛮高昂的。

I'm not sure. But storing personal items with the drinksmith isn't free... Until they pick up their belongings, a customer has to pay the World's End Tavern <unbreak>3,000</unbreak> credits and one original joke each day. That's quite a steep price.
看在TA这么执着的份上，就算「礼轻情意重」，您想必也可以通过讲个好故事，将这套纪念卡卖出高价吧。
Given their dedication, even if "it's the thought that counts," you can surely spin a good story and sell this commemorative card set for a hefty price.
不管怎么说，这里还有客人留下的一封信，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
Anyway, there's a letter here left by a guest. You can take your time reading it. I'll get back to my own work now.
不是说这人还留了一封信吗？
Didn't they say this person left a letter or something?
是啊！就在这里，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
Yep! Right here. Take your time reading it. I'll get back to my own work now.
欢迎游玩「星轨之兔」！「星轨之兔」是由月眠一族赞助的超高难度闯关游戏。您可以通过在附近集齐我同类的方式来降低游戏难度，从而轻松通关~

Welcome to Star Rabbit! Star Rabbit is an ultra-challenging obstacle course sponsored by the moonlulls. You can lower the difficulty by finding other rabbits nearby, making it easier to breeze through the levels~
「星轨之兔」的主题是「现实、努力、胜利」。您会发现我同类的言行举止，或许比普通的尘灵更加忧郁…但这正是烬土纪元古弁才天国的精神气质。

The theme of Star Rabbit is "Reality, Effort, Victory." You may find that those of my kind speak and behave in a manner that is perhaps more melancholic than ordinary wispae... but that is just the spiritual character of ancient Benzaitengoku from the Cindearth Age.
那时的英雄喜欢说，「适度的悲观是一种远见」。制作团队认为，基于悲观的英雄主义，这正是今天的哈托彼亚，或者说二相乐园——最稀缺的一种欢愉。

The heroes of that time liked to say, "A moderate dose of pessimism is foresight." The production team believes that this kind of heroism born from pessimism is precisely the rarest form of Elation in today's Ahatopia — or rather, Planarcadia.
如您所知，您可以通过在附近集齐我同类的方式来降低「星轨之兔」的游戏难度。为了给这个过程增添烬土纪元的风味，制作者对尘灵们的台词做了格言化处理。

As you know, you can reduce the difficulty of the Star Rabbit minigame by gathering my fellow wispae nearby. To add a bit of Cindearth Age flavor to the process, the creator has stylized the wispae's dialogue into aphorisms.
这并不代表在烬土纪元，英雄们出口就是格言…反而只有当他们慨然赴死的时候，才会如此这般去思考吧。
That's not to say heroes of the Cindearth Age spouted profound quotes left and right... Rather, they only thought this way when facing their final moments with resolve.
由于赞助商的存在，「星轨之兔」不必刻意迎合欢愉信徒们过于多变的时尚潮流。
Thanks to its sponsors, Star Rabbit doesn't have to chase after the ever-changing fashion trends of the Elation's followers.
或许尘灵们的言行与如今的二相乐园显得格格不入…但赞助商希望您能够相信，在古老的时代，沉思并不是一件会令人蒙羞的行为。
The wispae's behavior may seem out of place in modern-day Planarcadia, but the sponsors would like you to know that in ancient times, contemplation wasn't something to be ashamed of.
「星轨之兔」的背景设定在烬土纪元——这是在今天的二相乐园是一个经典的创作题材，但赞助商月眠一族希望制作组能为它赋予一些新意。
Star Rabbit is set against the backdrop of the Cindearth Age: a classic creative theme in today's Planarcadia. But the moonlull sponsors hope the production team can bring in some fresh ideas.
如今的时代剧或类似作品中，人们常常强调入画前人们的古典气息…但换个角度观察，那个时代也正是幻造种得以确立社会地位、哈托彼亚开始向现代发展的起点。

In period dramas and similar works, people often emphasize the classical atmosphere of the era before teleportation... but from another angle, that was also the very era when the imagenae established their social status and Ahatopia began its journey toward modernization.
基于这个视角，我们提供给您用来收集的尘灵，会有意强调它们作为现代人而非古人的侧面~
From this point of view, the wispae we're giving you to collect will deliberately highlight their modernity rather than their antiquity~
真没办法，就向您指出我某位同类的所在之处吧。下不为例哦？
Fine, fine, I'll point you toward one of my kin. But this is a one-time deal, got it?
您已经在附近找齐了我的同类…请在努力在游戏中活用我们的能力哦~
You've already collected all of my kind in this area... Now put our abilities to good use, okay~?
唔…降低难度需要支付代价。你做好准备了吗？<unbreak>20</unbreak>个像素金币，换一个降低难度的线索。
Hmm... lowering the difficulty comes at a cost. Are you ready? <unbreak>20</unbreak> Pixcoins for a hint to make things easier.
哎呀。平常请留意收集吧。
Oops. Better keep an eye out for them then.
尘灵用一点点愿力向你的手机中注入了一条导航信息。
The wispae used a bit of Wishpower to inject navigation info into your phone.
这需要一步一步来。先找到我上次为您指出的某位同类吧？
Let's take this one step at a time. First, how about finding that unit I pointed out to you last time?
Is there a reward for finding ten wispae?
…哦？您已经遇见了它们其中的一半？可喜可贺，请收下这份纪念奖励。
...Oh? You've already encountered half of them? Well, well, congratulations are in order! Please accept this commemorative reward.
Is there a reward for finding twenty wispae?
…哦？您已经全部遇见了它们？太厉害了，现在您游玩「星轨之兔」应该已经易如反掌~
...Oh? You've already encountered all of them? Impressive! Playing Star Rabbit should be a piece of cake for you now~
Oh, hey there! Long time no see, {NICKNAME}.
事不宜迟，先把东西交给你——
Let's get down to business. Here!
这次是一张关于「左脚」的卡牌。
This time, it's a card featuring a "left foot."
之前在短信里也说了，这次跑来寄存周边的人急匆匆就走了，什么多余的话都没有说，显得有些心不在焉…也不知道他们「星轨之兔」是不是出了什么变故。唔，也可能只是人家太忙了？

Like I said in my message, the person who dropped off the merch left in a huge hurry. They didn't say a single word, almost like their mind was elsewhere... I hope everything's okay with the Star Rabbit crew. Or maybe they're just super busy?
Being busy is great! No need to socialize!
哈哈。原来如此，原来如此。无名客做好事不留名的真正原因终于揭晓——因为不擅长社交！
Hah! Mystery solved! So that's why the Nameless stay anonymous. It's not about being humble, you're all just socially anxious!
咳咳，开个玩笑，别介意。我不知道他们的上封信里给你写了些什么…如果给你造成了社交疲劳，那么我觉得你还是要稍微原谅一下他们。毕竟是那是月眠一族嘛。
Ahem, just kidding! I don't know what they wrote in their last letter, but if it was a bit much for you, try to cut them some slack. They are moonlulls, after all.
他们里面有人仰慕无名客，那太正常不过了。
It's totally normal for them to admire the Nameless.
对吧对吧，你也有不好的预感？
Right? You've got a bad feeling, too?
真的吗…我直觉一向很准的哦。
Really? My intuition is usually spot on, you know.
不过话说回来，我也没有人家的联系方式，不太可能搞清楚到底发生了什么。
But I don't have any way to contact them, so I guess we'll never know what actually happened.
从你也一头雾水的角度来看…要想知道个中秘辛，或许要再等等吧。不管怎么说，我要继续忙自己的工作啦——你要能点杯饮料，那我就更开心啦。
Since you're in the dark too, I guess the truth will just have to wait. Anyway, I've got to get back to work. I'd be a lot happier if you could order a drink, though.
本作是基于赞助商搜集的民间传说定制而成。也就是说，这不是一份关于二相乐园历史事实的记录，而是一份关于二相乐园历史情绪的记录。
This work is a commissioned adaptation based on folklore assembled by our sponsors. It is, therefore, not a document of Planarcadia's historical facts, but of its historical emotional record.
…请不要小看历史情绪。在时间的长河中，比起历史事实，历史情绪才是历史作用于现实的直接力量。也因此才有「一切历史都是当代史」的提法。

...Please do not underestimate historical emotion. In time's long flow, it is not the cold facts but the living emotions of the past that shape our present. This is the very reason why we have the saying, "All history is contemporary history."
呃，我们一定要聊这个吗？好吧，这的确是我的责任之一…之前应该已经有很多尘灵给你讲过各种各样的八卦了……
Ugh, do we have to talk about this? Fine, I guess it is one of my responsibilities... You've probably already heard all kinds of gossip from the other wispae...
啊，有了。月眠一族一直声称自己是星空旅行家的后代——但他们从来都拒绝幻造种溯源检测，也就是说拒绝用科学方法验证此事的真假。嗯，你细品。

Ah, here it is. The moonlulls have always claimed to be descendants of starry voyagers, but they always refuse the Imagenae Retracing Test. In other words, they refuse to scientifically verify whether this claim is true or false. Hmm... just think about the implications.
我又找到了两页《浮脂记事》。
I found two more pages of the Floating Grease Chronicles.
真的？那我要趁自己还活着，赶紧一睹为快……
Really? Then I better check it out while I'm still kicking...
查令在手机上读到了「兔之章 其五」和「猴之章 其十一」。
Charing read "Chapter of the Rabbit (V)" and "Chapter of the Monkey (XI)" on the phone.
…没想到我们虽然收齐了四大篇章的「其一」，后面的内容却如此残缺不全。
...I can't believe it. Even though we managed to collect Part I for all four chapters, the rest of the content is so fragmented and incomplete.
本来我还希望在「其二」或者「其三」中能找到一些关于幻月诞生的蛛丝马迹…但现在看来，这套诗集的保存状况比我之前的设想要糟糕很多。
I was hoping to find some clues about the birth of the Phantasmoon in Part II or Part III... but the collection's in way worse shape than I thought.
至少单篇诗作还是完整的。
At least the individual poems are still intact.
嗯，这一点确实万幸。不过……
Yeah, that's a relief, at least. But...
说不定只是书页被打乱了顺序？
Maybe the pages just got shuffled around?
按照我之前的推理，页码越大的部分越容易从幻造书上脱落——这也是为什么我们能在离它最近的地方发现「序章」和四大篇章的「其一」——因为它们是最后脱落的。

Following my earlier deduction, the higher the page number, the more likely that section is to fall off the imagenesis book. That would explain why we found the "Prologue" and the "Part I" of the four major chapters closest to the book itself. They were the last to fall off.
但之后，我们找到的却已经是「兔之章 其五」和「猴之章 其十一」，这就意味着它们之间的内容，很可能在许多许多年前就已经散佚了。
But after that, what we found were the "Chapter of the Rabbit (V)" and "Chapter of the Monkey (XI)." That means the content in between had likely been lost many, many years ago.
…不过，这或许也说明，已经很久、很久没有人试图整理、抢救《浮脂记事》了——只要我们能搜集到足够重新出版的数量，那一定会有人高价收购！

...But hey, maybe that just means it's been ages, like eons, since anyone tried to catalog or salvage the Floating Grease Chronicles. If we can collect enough copies to reprint it, someone's bound to pay big for them!
呃…大概是这样吧。其实我也不确定二相乐园的人们对于古籍还有多大的热情。我的旧书店里面，销量好的还都是漫画书呢。
Uh... I guess so. Honestly, I'm not even sure how much the folks in Planarcadia still care about ancient texts. At my used bookstore, it's always comic books that sell.
哈哈，借你吉言。要是机缘巧合，旧书店说不定也能用自己的方式拯救世界呢！
Haha, I'll hope that's true! Who knows? Maybe with a bit of luck, this little bookstore of mine can save the world in its own way!
单纯当作探险也挺好玩。
Just thinking of it as an adventure is fun too.
确实！对你来说，寻找书页本身就是一种探险游戏了。
Exactly! For you, finding those pages is an adventure in itself.
This is what they call "times have changed."
是啊。想当初，像这样的诗歌，又何尝不是那个年代的流行文化呢。
Yeah. Back in the day, poetry like this was exactly what pop culture is today.
这样想想，就算我们最后收集到的《浮脂记事》残破不堪，根本无法体现原作的完整面貌…它也一定会有自己的独特意义。

When you put it that way, even if what we end up with is an incomplete collection of the Floating Grease Chronicles, a far cry from the original work in its full glory... it'll still carry its own unique weight.
谢谢你！只要我还没死掉，那下次我们就还要继续合作哦？
Thanks! As long as I'm still alive, we'll be working together again next time, yeah?
裂界中一片死寂。粘滞的空气几乎在这座废弃电话亭的周围结成了块状，你感觉自己像被淹没在融化过程中的棉花糖里，喘不上气来。
All's quiet in the Fragmentum. You feel like you're drowning in gooey cotton candy in the stagnant air that seems to congeal around the phone booth.
——棉花糖？嗯…借着这一闪而过的比喻，你开始想象棉花糖的味道了——虽然你不确定自己是否真的尝试过这种食物。或许你该去问问那位列车长……
Cotton Candy, eh...? You begin imagining how it would taste based on the brief description, even though you're not sure if you've ever had it. Perhaps you should ask the conductor...
等等，那是什么声音？一阵诡异的电话铃声撕破了凝滞的空气。你脑海里的抽象棉花糖被一口吞掉，甚至没给你留下一丝回甘。
Wait, what's that sound? The phone rings, shattering the silence and ripping the cotton candy from your mind, not even leaving an aftertaste.
摆在你面前的只剩一个问题：接还是不接？
Only one question remains... Are you picking up the phone or not?
I got no choice but to answer.
不错！身为开拓者，你怎么能连这点冒险精神都没有？
Yes! No true Trailblazer should ignore such an adventure!
去吧，拿起那个…肮脏的听筒！听听这裂界到底想用什么故事刺激你的神经。
Now, pick up that dirty phone and hear what stimulating story the Fragmentum has in store for you!
不…这完全不明智。别碰它。
No... this is a bad idea. Not going to touch it.
感性带来的冲动悄然退场，理智宣布了它的胜利。只是又一个裂界描摹现实产生的幻象罢了，它不值得你浪费宝贵的时间。
The impulse fades and sensibility takes over. It's just another Fragmentum illusion not worth your time.
——或许更主要的原因还是那个肮脏的听筒。那玩意对你的听觉肯定没益处……
Or maybe it's the filthy phone. Nothing good can come out of touching it...
你进来了！后悔已经晚了。假如你现在逃跑，所有人都会嘲笑你的。
You're already inside and it's too late for regrets! You'll be mocked if you run away now.
你拿起了听筒，小心翼翼地把它贴到耳边……
You pick up the phone and carefully put it next to your ear...
听筒中传来一个陌生女人的声音。出乎你的意料，她的声音出奇地正常。她听上去只是有些衰弱。
You're not familiar with the female voice. Surprisingly, the voice sounds calm, just a bit weak.
她又重复了一遍。她在渴求回答。
我在…贝洛伯格的商业街上。
I'm... in the business district of Belobog.
别回来了…我不想见到你。
Don't come back. I don't want to see you.
碧翠丝哭了一整晚。但我知道你根本不在乎。
Beatrice cried the whole night, but I know you don't care.
我累了。我要去睡一会儿…我不想再争吵了。我没有力气……
I'm tired and want to go to bed. I don't want to fight anymore... I don't have the energy...
她很生气…你不知道为什么。你什么都没做，但罪恶感还是像苏打水翻腾的气泡一样，在你胸腔里滋滋作响。
She's livid but you don't even know why. You didn't do anything, but guilt still filled your chest like bubbling soda water.
你不明白。这么多年了，你从来就没真正反省过……
All these years, you never once blamed yourself...
你眼里就只有生意…为了那一摊子生意，你什么都能放弃……
All you do is sell... You'd give up anything for the business...
如果…如果你觉得我们之间还有重归于好的余地…那你真是被冲昏了头。
If... If you think we can still reconcile, you're out of your mind.
你终于意识到了…那个女人并不是在与你对话。她的语句间之所以留有间隙，纯粹是因为一阵又一阵的情感溃堤。
You finally realize the woman is not interested in a conversation so much as venting her frustrations.
那就保持沉默吧。听听她——也许是个幽灵——还有什么话想说。
Just be quiet and listen. Maybe she's a spirit with something to say.
我要离开了。带着碧翠丝一起…去哪儿？我不知道，也不会告诉你。但总之不是这里……
I'm leaving and taking Beatrice with me. Where to? I don't know and won't tell you, but I won't stay here anymore...
我真希望贝洛伯格能再大一些…那样我就不用担心会再碰见你了。
I wish Belobog could be larger, so I don't chance running into you.
沉默。沉默。一阵漫长到令你难以忍受的沉默。
Stay quiet, though it's becoming unbearable.
顺带一提，昨天是碧翠丝的五岁生日。二月十三日。
By the way, Beatrice turned five yesterday, February <unbreak>13</unbreak>.
记不住也没关系…反正以后也和你无关了。再见，史考——
It's okay if you don't remember, not that you need to, anymore. So long, Sco—
电话断线了。聒噪的「嘟嘟」声不断敲击着你空白的心灵。
The call is disconnected. The annoying beeps gnaw at your soul.
（那个女人最后提到了一个名字…「史考」？听着不像是个完整的名字。）
(She mentioned the name "Sco—" at the end, but it didn't sound complete...)
（还有其它值得注意的信息吗？对了，她还提到了他们的女儿…碧翠丝？而且她的生日是二月十三日…）
(Anything else worth noting... Oh, she mentioned their daughter... Beatrice? Her birthday was February <unbreak>13</unbreak>.)
（不知道这些信息有什么意义…总之先记下来吧。）
(Don't know what that's good for, but I'll write it down, anyway.)
桌子上放着一只老旧的箱子，它的开口被比箱子本身看上去更为老旧的机械锁锁住。
There's an old box on the table with a combination lock that appears even older than the box.
桌子旁放着一张纸条，上面用潦草的字体写着：「史考特先生的箱子。没有值钱的东西。勿动」——哈！真是此地无银三百两。
Next to it is a memo with sloppy handwriting that reads, "Mr. Scott's box, containing nothing valuable inside. Do not open." Aha! He couldn't have advertised its value more clearly.
该不该试着打开它呢？老实说，那个史考特的态度奇差…你早就看他不爽了。
Should you open it? Frankly, you don't like Scott as it is...
Try to figure out the combination.
没错，动手吧！就算发生了什么，也都是那个惹人厌的拳馆老板自找的——至少你能这么安慰自己。
Sure, do it! It's all that gym owner's fault, anyway... At least that's your excuse.
你强行压抑住了内心的冲动。没错，人之所以为人，就是因为我们心中有值得奉行的道德律——
You suppress the urge. That's right, we are humans because we know right from wrong...
——算了，别把话说的太死。也许下一秒你就改主意了呢？
Come on, don't be too sure of it. You could be having a change of heart the next second.
你把手放到了箱子上。这个老旧的机械锁一共有四个凹槽，每个凹槽对应一个数字。现在轮到你行动了——第一个数字是什么？
You put your hands on the box. The ancient combo lock has four slots, which corresponds to four numbers. Now it's your turn. So, what's the first number?
咔哒！清脆的声音从机械锁中响起。看来你猜对了第一个数字。
*click* The lock clanged loudly and crisply. You've guessed the first number.
错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。

哟，这不是…西蒙勋爵给我的那块表嘛！我正纳闷这玩意跑哪去了呢，谢谢你啊。

So no recompense for returning the watch to you?
当然了，我华劳斯怎么会亏待朋友呢！

你就不好奇它是怎么丢的吗？
So, you aren't even slightly curious about how it got lost?
不好奇啊。就是个第三任大守护者的遗物而已嘛，丢了也不是很可惜。
Not really. It's just an artifact left behind by the third Supreme Guardian. Won't be that big of a loss anyway.
不过，既然你帮我找到了，那我肯定要如约重谢——我华劳斯绝不会亏待朋友！

这是我一点心意，还请笑纳。
This is the reward I prepared for you. Please enjoy.
啊，失败了吗…人们都说「一叶障目，不见幻月」，原来用两片叶子是不行的啊。我真傻，老板。

当然您是我的老板呀！我在《狸狸周刊》勤勤恳恳工作了十三年，只是进酒馆的机会太难得，这才一时鬼迷心窍……
You are, of course! I've worked hard at Furbobo Weekly for thirteen years. It's just... opportunities to sneak into the tavern are rare, and I let temptation get the better of me...
Oh, so you work for the magazine too...
是的老板，没错老板！我在《狸狸周刊》勤勤恳恳工作了十三年，只是进酒馆的机会太难得，这才一时鬼迷心窍……

是是是，老板您最好了！我这就回去上班！
Yes, yes, you're the best, boss! I'll head back right now!
Okay, I get it. Just this once.
啊啊啊，感谢老板慈悲为怀！下次我一定只用一片叶子——啊不是，下次我一定不敢了！我这就回去上班！
Ah, thank you for your mercy, boss! Next time I'll only use one leaf... No, wait, I mean I won't do it again! I'll go right now!
啊啊啊，老板饶命啊！我全家老小都指着这一口饭，下次一定不敢了啊！
Ahhh, please forgive me, boss! My whole family depends on this job! I won't do it again, I swear!
I'll be merciful, if only to gain furbo allegiance.
感谢老板，感谢老板！下次我一定只用一片叶子——啊不是，下次我一定不敢了！我这就回去上班！
Thank you, boss! Thank you! Next time I'll only use one leaf... No, wait, I mean I won't do it again! I'll go right now!
完了…全完了…如果还有下次，我一定只用一片叶子……
It's over... It's all over... If I ever get another chance, I'll only use one leaf...
{NICKNAME}，真的是你？大家都说星穹列车也来二相乐园了，我正打算要找你和三月七玩，没想到会在「酒馆」先碰面。
{NICKNAME}? It really is you! I heard the Astral Express made its way to Planarcadia, and I was planning to find you and March 7th to hang out. Didn't expect to run into you here at the tavern!
Yunli? What are you doing here?
笨，这还用想，我当然是跟着爻老板的玉阙使团一起来的呀！
Isn't it obvious? I came along with Madam Yao's Yuque delegation!
这可不是小孩子该来的地方。
This isn't really a place for kids.
那块牌子不是只说不让阿哈和悲悼伶人进来吗？而且，我可是爻老板的玉阙使团成员。
Didn't that sign only say Aha and the Mourning Actors aren't allowed in? Besides, I'm a member of Madam Yao's Yuque delegation.
师父他老人家都说啦，练万次剑不如行万里路，这回跟着爻老板来增长见识，我想着来都来了，不如多去几个平时见不到的地方，多看点新鲜事。
Master always says that ten thousand miles of travel beats ten thousand swings of the sword. So I figured, since I'm here, I might as well see a few new places and experience something fresh.
别说，这二相乐园<color=#dbc291ff>特色料狸</color>是真的好吃，我这几天尝了无梦咖啡，狮子头，滋味不输仙舟的琼实鸟串。

You know, the <color=#dbc291ff>special furdishes</color> here in Planarcadia are amazing! I've tried Dreamless Coffee and meatball these past few days, and they're just as good as Xianzhou's berrypheasant skewers.
黄金告解室的人也都特别好玩，个个都<color=#dbc291ff>很有狸貌</color>，说话又好听……
The folks at the Golden Confessional are all hilarious. They're <color=#dbc291ff>so bolite</color>, and they talk so smoothly...
哼哼，居然被老板识破了，我只好在此奉上本名——不讲狸！
Heh heh, looks like you've caught me, boss. Guess I'll have to reveal my true name, Fureasonabo!
狸狸我专门休了年假来这里玩，还特意施展了变身术，让这个难得的假期更有节目效果，可惜被老板您打断了。
I took a vacation to come have fun, even cast a transformation spell to make it more interesting, but you went and interrupted me, boss.
既然老板打断了狸狸的节目，那么就让您自己变成节目的一部分吧！
Since you've interrupted my act, you'll just have to be part of the show!
可恶…好狠心的老板，我爸爸都没有打过我……
Ugh, boss, how heartless... even my dad never hit me...
嗯？不对，我是从流水线上画出来的，哪来的爸爸？
Wait, that's not right. I was drawn on an assembly line, so I don't have a dad!
不讲狸要换个地方思考这些不讲理的问题，再见！想要我回杂志社工作，等年假休完再说哩！
I must find somewhere to ponder these nonsensical questions. Bye! If you want me back at the magazine, wait until my vacation's over!
嗯嗯，照相也很需要节奏感呢！
Mhm, taking photos really is all about rhythm!
Hehe, you'll never catch me, boss!
啊…还是被抓到啦。老板老板，我这个变成路障的想法是不是很有创意？快夸夸我！
Ah... you caught me again. Boss, boss, isn't my idea of turning into a roadblock super creative? Say something nice!
哦，原来你也是杂志社的…
Oh, so you work for the magazine too...
是啊是啊。怎么样，我是不是比其他员工更有创意？
Yup! So, what do you think? I'm way more creative than the others, right?
哦，没事没事，不用在意！在这小巷子里，您我不是我的老板，我也不是您的员工，咱们就是玩了一场捉迷藏而已！
Oh, don't worry about it! Here in this alley, you're not my boss, and I'm not your employee. We just had a round of hide-and-seek!
嘿嘿嘿嘿，感谢老板，咱们下回再见~
Hehe, thanks, boss. Let's do this again sometime~
